content:
Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,966.30, up 48.60 points or 0.54%, hitting a new 20-day high. The index has now gained 3.62% over five sessions and sits just 2.57% off its 52-week high. Technology and gold-linked names drove the advance as risk appetite returned to the market. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 2
Information Technology led all sectors, gaining 2.03%, while Energy was the clear laggard, down 2.26%. Karoon Energy fell 13.37% to AUD 1.425, the sharpest single-stock decline of the session, with Woodside dropping a further 3.60% to AUD 28.960. On the other side, Web Travel surged 11.07% and SiteMinder added 10.10%. #ASX #stocks

Post 3
Gold is the level to watch, currently priced at AUD 6,136.76 per oz. Three of the top five ASX 200 gainers were gold miners, with Resolute, Pantoro, and Catalyst all rising between 6.80% and 8.60%. Any pullback in the AUD gold price from current levels would test whether that sector momentum holds into the back half of the week. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,966.30, up 48.60 points or 0.54%, hitting a 20-day high. A five-day rally of 3.62% keeps the index within 2.57% of its 52-week high. Tech and materials led the charge as risk appetite held firm. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 2
IT gained 2.03% while Energy shed 2.26%, the session's sharpest divergence. Karoon Energy fell 13.37% to AUD 1.425, the index's worst performer, while Web Travel surged 11.07% to AUD 3.010. Sector rotation away from energy was the defining theme. #ASX #ausbiz

Post 3
Gold at AUD 6,134.15 per oz with AUD/USD at 0.7052 is the key read heading into Thursday. A firmer Australian dollar compresses gold returns in local terms — watch whether AUD/USD holds above 0.70 as the line that matters for materials sentiment. #ASX #ausbiz
